title: NOC 60040 – Other Customer and Personal Services Managers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 60040 covers a broad and vital spectrum of Canada’s service economy, including the management of spas, salons, cleaning services, and specialized schools. These managers ensure quality and customer satisfaction across the nation's personal service establishments. As a TEER 0 managerial role, it offers a distinct advantage for immigration, allowing creative and operationally-minded leaders to secure their PR while contributing to Canada’s vibrant community life.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level0Major Group60 – Executive and senior management occupationsSub-Major Group600 – Management occupationsMinor Group6004 – Managers in customer and personal servicesNOC Code60040

duties: Plan, direct, and evaluate the operations of establishments providing personal services.
Manage budgets, oversee spending, and set pricing strategies.
Recruit and train staff and monitor their performance.
Resolve customer complaints and represent the business to the public.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or vocational certificate in the subject of instruction (for schools) may be required.
One to three years of experience in the specific service being offered are usually required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Continued growth in the health, wellness, and specialized education sectors will keep demand consistent, particularly in the Mainland/Southwest region of British Columbia and the Greater Toronto Area.
labour_market_demand: The demand for personal service managers is rising alongside Canada’s urban population. There is a particularly high need for managers in the wellness industry (spas/salons) and in specialized education (language and driving schools). Self-employment is also high in this NOC, offering opportunities for entrepreneurial immigrants.
High Self-Employment Rates: Almost 48% of managers in this category are self-employed.
Diverse Industries: Flexibility to work in beauty, education, or residential services.
Strong Community Impact: Managing services that directly enhance the lives of Canadians.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: For those with a significant track record of managing their own service business.
Express Entry: High ranking for TEER 0 managerial experience.
Saskatchewan INP: Targeted draws for managers in demand.
